{{Quote|Final call, freighter ''Star of Iskin''. Passengers must complete their boarding now.|Coruscant spaceport announcement|<ref name="Republic61" />}}
The '''''Star of Iskin''''' was a [[Botajef Shipyards/Legends|Botajef Shipyards]] [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|AA-9 freighter]] operational during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], sixteen [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|beginning]] of the war, it was scheduled to depart the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with passengers and cargo bound for the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. [[Politics/Legends|Political]] [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] and former [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] planned to use the [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]] as [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] to distance [[Gender/Legends|himself]] from the political intrigue of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]].

However, upon its departure from Coruscant on [[Great ReSynchronization|14:9:19]], the ''Star of Iskin'' [[Terrorist attack of 14:9:19|exploded]], destroyed as part of a successful plot carried out by [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Sajé Tasha]] to [[Death/Legends|murder]] Valorum. All aboard the freighter were killed in what the general public viewed as an act of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorism]] engineered by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]; thousands more lives were claimed when the wreckage crashed down upon the [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] [[Galactic City/Legends|cityscape]]. The ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction was used to great effect by certain [[Senator/Legends|members]] of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to pass the controversial [[Enhanced Security and Enforcement Act]], a piece of legislation that delegated additional wartime powers to Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Valorum's political opponent.

==Description==
A 390-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long<ref name="CSWE3" /> [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|AA-9 Coruscant freighter]]<ref name="CSWE1" /> manufactured by the [[Botajef Shipyards/Legends|Botajef Shipyards]],<ref name="CSWE2" /> the ''Star of Iskin'' was a [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]] that ferried cargo and passengers from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. It had three [[Engine/Legends|engines]].<ref name="Republic61" />

==History==
===Final voyage===
{{Quote|…this terrible act of sabotage, the ''sin'' of terrorism, that destroyed the ''Star of Iskin'', killing not only all those board, but thousands more who died when it came to fall on the city!|Senator Ask Aak, before the Galactic Senate|<ref name="Republic61" />}}
[[File:Iskin2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Onlookers watch in horror as the ''Star of Iskin'' explodes.]]
The [[Transport/Legends|transport]] ''Star of Iskin''<ref name="Republic61" /> was in service by [[21 BBY/Legends|20.65 BBY]], sixteen [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|opening battle]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="NEC" /> On [[Great ReSynchronization|14:9:19]],<ref name="HNN" /> the freighter was scheduled to depart a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on Coruscant with a mixture of passengers and cargo aboard. Among the travelers planning to use the ''Star of Iskin'' to journey [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Rimward]] was [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]], the former Republic [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] who had returned from a self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] to assist [[Senator/Legends|Senators]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] and [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] in opposing current Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] increasing wartime powers.<ref name="Republic61" />

However, opponents of Valorum's had planned to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the former leader of the Republic. [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Sajé Tasha]] was hired by the corrupt Senator [[Viento]] and Separatist [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|acolyte]] [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] to eliminate Valorum.<ref name="Republic73">[[Republic 73|''Republic'' 73]]</ref> After the disgraced [[Politics/Legends|politician]] boarded the ''Star of Iskin'',<ref name="Republic61" /> Tasha killed Valorum and drank his "[[Soup (Anzati)|soup]]," per Anzati tradition, before [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaging]] and escaping the freighter. As a result of Tasha's actions,<ref name="Republic73" /> the ''Star of Iskin'' [[Terrorist attack of 14:9:19|exploded]] shortly after takeoff. All aboard perished, and thousands more were killed when the freighter's wreckage fell back to [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]].<ref name="Republic61" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|In the days following the tragic and cowardly attack that claimed the life of this man, the news of our loss was overshadowed by the security policies that it spawned. But Finis Valorum would never have backed such extreme reactionary measures and would have seen them as affronts to the freedoms he held dear.|Bail Organa, speaking at the dedication of the Finis Valorum Memorial|<ref name="HNN" />}}
[[File:Valorum_During_The_Clone_Wars.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Disgraced politician Finis Valorum was the true target in the ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction.]]
The ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction was viewed as an act of terrorism,<ref name="Republic61" /> and the attack was ultimately attributed to the Separatists.<ref name="CSWE4">{{CSWECite|III|293|Valorum, Supreme Chancellor Finis}}</ref> [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] Senator [[Ask Aak/Legends|Ask Aak]] cited the Republic's inability to prevent the freighter's destruction as proof that the [[Enhanced Security and Enforcement Act]], a controversial piece of legislation that would grant Palpatine more authority at the expense of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], was desperately needed. Despite Senator Organa's best efforts to block the bill, the Senate passed the act the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] after the freighter exploded, giving the Chancellor greater wartime powers—that which Finis Valorum had fought against.<ref name="Republic61" /> Additional policies, such as the [[Core Worlds Security Act]], were also passed in the wake of the disaster.<ref name="HNN" />

Immediately after the ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction, a [[Finis Valorum Memorial|memorial]] for the late Valorum was planned for the [[Chancellery Walkway]] in Coruscant's [[Jrade District/Legends|Jrade District]], although, amidst security concerns, the memorial was not opened to the public until [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] later.<ref name="HNN" /> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] later tracked down Tasha. Using his [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometric]] [[Force power/Legends|abilities]], the [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was able to witness the Anzat's memories of Valorum's murder and the ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction.<ref name="Republic73" /> Organa became increasingly suspicious of Palpatine in the months following the ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction and, with Senator Mothma, began forming a [[Delegation of 2000|political body]] opposed to the Chancellor's increasing influence.<ref name="NEC" /> By [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> just prior to the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], Organa still viewed Valorum's assassination as one of many convenient incidences that had occurred during Palpatine's consolidation of power.<ref name="Labyrinth">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Star of Iskin'' first appeared in [[Republic 61|''Dead Ends'']], the sixty-first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]],<ref name="Republic61" /> released on [[February 18]], [[2004]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54509/star_wars_republic_2002_61|text=Star Wars: Republic (2002) #61}}</ref> "[[The Lost One]]," an episode of the [[The Clone Wars: The Lost Missions|sixth season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, shows Finis Valorum alive and well<ref name="Lost One">{{TCW|The Lost One}}</ref> in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="FF-2014 60: Sifo-Dyas">{{FactFile2014|60|20 BBY 67-70|Sifo-Dyas' Legacy}}</ref> in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period following the ''Star of Iskin''{{'s}} destruction in 21 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="NEC" />
